message:
fix(core): guard chunked INSERT...SELECT against self-modifying queries

The LIMIT/OFFSET chunking added in 138979c6 could produce incorrect
results for self-modifying queries (INSERT INTO t SELECT FROM t) where
each chunk's SELECT would see rows inserted by prior chunks.

Fall back to single-shot materialization when:
 1. The SELECT already has a LIMIT clause
 2. The INSERT has CTEs (WITH clause) that may reference the target
    table through indirection
 3. The INSERT target table appears in the SELECT's source tables

Chunking remains active for the common case that triggers OOM: INSERT
into a different table (e.g., FTS repair: INSERT INTO fts SELECT FROM
main_table) where source and destination are disjoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
